description_long: This sculpture depicts a wild turkey in mid-flight, wings fully extended and body angled upward as if taking off from a tree stump. The bird is rendered with detailed feather textures in shades of brown, gray, and tan, with a red wattle and a pale beak. The base of the sculpture is shaped and painted to resemble a patch of ground with a realistic tree stump, adding to the naturalistic setting. The words 'WILD TURKEY' are inscribed on the base, clearly identifying the subject. The overall form is dynamic, capturing the energy and movement of the bird. The sculpture appears to be made of painted ceramic or resin, with a matte finish and fine surface detailing.
description: A detailed sculpture of a wild turkey taking flight from a tree stump, with outstretched wings and realistic painted textures.
description_view_0: The left side of the turkey is visible, showing the full span of the left wing and the inscription 'WILD TURKEY' on the base.
description_view_1: The rear view highlights the back of the turkey and the tree stump, with the tail feathers and stump details most prominent.
description_view_2: The right side of the turkey is shown, with the right wing fully extended and the head and beak clearly visible.
description_view_3: The back right angle reveals the turkey's tail and the rear of the tree stump, with less visibility of the head and wings.
attribute_location_context: No specific installation context is visible; the sculpture could be placed in a park, garden, or indoor display.
